title: Infectious gastroenteritis and colitis
standard_treatment: Oral rehydration salts, bland diet. Loperamide 2mg after each loose stool (max 16mg/day). Zinc 20mg daily for 10-14 days. Antibiotics only if bacterial confirmed.
notes: Usually viral (rotavirus, norovirus). Stool culture if bloody or persistent.

title: Tuberculosis of lung
standard_treatment: RIPE regimen: Rifampicin 600mg + Isoniazid 300mg + Pyrazinamide 25mg/kg + Ethambutol 15mg/kg daily for 2 months, then Rifampicin + Isoniazid for 4 months.
notes: Notify public health. Check HIV status. Monitor liver function on RIPE therapy.

title: Viral infection, unspecified
standard_treatment: Supportive care: rest, fluids, acetaminophen 500-1000mg q6h for fever/pain. Monitor for secondary bacterial infection.
notes: Most viral infections are self-limiting. Antivirals only for specific confirmed viruses.

title: Iron deficiency anemia
standard_treatment: Ferrous sulfate 325mg (65mg elemental iron) PO twice daily on empty stomach with vitamin C. Treat underlying cause.
notes: Check ferritin, serum iron, TIBC. Rule out GI bleeding in adults. Response expected in 2-4 weeks.

title: Type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications
standard_treatment: Metformin 500mg BID, titrate to 1000mg BID. Lifestyle: diet modification, 150min/week exercise. HbA1c target <7%.
notes: Screen for complications: retinopathy, nephropathy, neuropathy annually. Check HbA1c every 3 months.

title: Hypothyroidism, unspecified
standard_treatment: Levothyroxine 25-50mcg daily, increase by 25mcg every 4-6 weeks. Take on empty stomach 30min before food. Target TSH 0.5-4.0 mU/L.
notes: Check TSH every 6-8 weeks during dose titration, then annually. Common cause: Hashimoto thyroiditis.

title: Hyperlipidemia, unspecified
standard_treatment: Atorvastatin 10-20mg daily at bedtime. Lifestyle: low saturated fat diet, exercise 150min/week, weight management. Target LDL <100mg/dL.
notes: Check lipid panel fasting. Recheck in 4-12 weeks after starting statin. Monitor liver enzymes.

title: Major depressive disorder, single episode
standard_treatment: SSRI first-line: Sertraline 50mg daily or Escitalopram 10mg daily. CBT recommended. Reassess in 4-6 weeks. Continue treatment 6-12 months after remission.
notes: Screen with PHQ-9. Rule out hypothyroidism, anemia, substance use. Warn about SSRI side effects in first 2 weeks.

title: Generalized anxiety disorder
standard_treatment: SSRI first-line: Sertraline 50mg or Escitalopram 10mg daily. Short-term benzodiazepine if severe: Lorazepam 0.5mg PRN (max 2 weeks). CBT recommended.
notes: Screen with GAD-7. Avoid long-term benzodiazepine use. Rule out hyperthyroidism, cardiac arrhythmia.

title: Migraine, unspecified
standard_treatment: Acute: Sumatriptan 50-100mg PO at onset, may repeat after 2h. Ibuprofen 400mg + Metoclopramide 10mg. Preventive if >4/month: Propranolol 40mg BID or Topiramate 25mg daily.
notes: Keep headache diary. Identify triggers. Avoid medication overuse (>10 days/month triptans, >15 days/month simple analgesics).

title: Insomnia
standard_treatment: Sleep hygiene first-line. CBT-I (cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia). If pharmacotherapy needed: Melatonin 3-5mg 30min before bed. Short-term Zolpidem 5-10mg if severe.
notes: Avoid benzodiazepines long-term. Screen for depression, anxiety, sleep apnea. Limit caffeine after noon.

title: Essential hypertension
standard_treatment: ACE inhibitor: Enalapril 5mg daily or ARB: Losartan 50mg daily. Lifestyle: DASH diet, sodium <2g/day, exercise 150min/week, weight loss. Target BP <130/80 mmHg.
notes: Confirm with 24h ambulatory monitoring. Check renal function, electrolytes, lipids. Screen for end-organ damage.

title: Angina pectoris, unspecified
standard_treatment: Acute: Sublingual GTN 0.4mg, repeat every 5min x3. Maintenance: Aspirin 75mg daily + Atorvastatin 40mg + Beta-blocker Metoprolol 25-50mg BID. Refer cardiology.
notes: ECG, troponin, lipid panel. Stress test or angiography. Risk factor modification essential.

title: Atherosclerotic heart disease
standard_treatment: Aspirin 75-100mg daily, Atorvastatin 40-80mg, ACE inhibitor, Beta-blocker. Lifestyle modification. Refer cardiology for assessment.
notes: Annual cardiology follow-up. Monitor lipids, BP, glucose. Cardiac rehabilitation if indicated.

title: Heart failure, unspecified
standard_treatment: ACE inhibitor + Beta-blocker + Diuretic. Enalapril 2.5-20mg BID + Carvedilol 3.125mg BID titrate up + Furosemide 20-40mg daily. Fluid restrict 1.5-2L/day. Salt restrict <2g/day.
notes: Echo for EF. BNP/NT-proBNP for monitoring. Daily weights. Refer cardiology.

title: Acute upper respiratory infection, unspecified
standard_treatment: Supportive: rest, fluids, acetaminophen 500-1000mg q6h for fever/pain. Honey for cough. Saline nasal spray. Usually resolves in 7-10 days.
notes: Usually viral — antibiotics NOT indicated. Consider strep throat if severe pharyngitis, test with rapid antigen.

title: Pneumonia, unspecified organism
standard_treatment: Community-acquired: Amoxicillin 500mg TID x 5 days. If atypical suspected: add Azithromycin 500mg day 1, then 250mg x 4 days. Moderate-severe: Amoxicillin-clavulanate 875/125mg BID.
notes: CXR to confirm. Check SpO2, CRP, CBC. Use CURB-65 for severity. Blood cultures if hospitalized. Consider TB in endemic areas.

title: Asthma, unspecified
standard_treatment: Step 1: SABA PRN (Salbutamol 100mcg 2 puffs). Step 2: Low-dose ICS (Beclometasone 100mcg BID). Step 3: Add LABA (Formoterol) or increase ICS. Always provide written action plan.
notes: Peak flow monitoring. Inhaler technique check at every visit. Identify triggers. Annual flu vaccine recommended.

title: COPD with acute exacerbation
standard_treatment: Bronchodilators: Salbutamol nebulizer 2.5mg q4h + Ipratropium 500mcg q6h. Prednisolone 40mg daily x 5 days. Antibiotics if purulent sputum: Amoxicillin 500mg TID x 5-7 days.
notes: Check SpO2, ABG if severe. CXR to rule out pneumonia/pneumothorax. Smoking cessation essential. Maintenance: long-acting bronchodilators + ICS.

title: Gastroesophageal reflux disease with esophagitis
standard_treatment: PPI: Omeprazole 20mg daily 30min before breakfast x 4-8 weeks. Lifestyle: elevate head of bed, avoid late meals, reduce caffeine/alcohol/spicy food, weight loss if overweight.
notes: Endoscopy if alarm symptoms or no response to PPI. Long-term PPI: consider calcium, magnesium, B12 monitoring.

title: Gastritis, unspecified
standard_treatment: PPI: Omeprazole 20mg daily x 4 weeks. H. pylori test — if positive: triple therapy (Omeprazole 20mg BID + Amoxicillin 1g BID + Clarithromycin 500mg BID x 14 days).
notes: Avoid NSAIDs, alcohol, smoking. Endoscopy if alarm features or age >55.

title: Acute appendicitis
standard_treatment: Surgical: appendectomy (laparoscopic preferred). Pre-op: NPO, IV fluids, IV antibiotics (Ceftriaxone 2g + Metronidazole 500mg). Pain: Morphine 2-4mg IV PRN.
notes: CT abdomen if diagnosis uncertain. Alvarado score for clinical assessment. Surgical emergency — do not delay.

title: Gallstone without cholecystitis
standard_treatment: Acute pain: Diclofenac 75mg IM or Ketorolac 30mg IV. Avoid fatty foods. Elective laparoscopic cholecystectomy if symptomatic.
notes: Ultrasound abdomen for diagnosis. Check LFTs, amylase. Urgent surgery if complicated.

title: Atopic dermatitis (eczema)
standard_treatment: Emollients liberally and frequently. Mild: Hydrocortisone 1% cream BID x 2 weeks. Moderate: Betamethasone 0.05% cream BID x 2 weeks. Antihistamine for itch: Cetirizine 10mg daily.
notes: Avoid triggers: soaps, wool, stress. Step-down steroids as improving. Refer dermatology if severe/unresponsive.

title: Urticaria (hives)
standard_treatment: Cetirizine 10mg daily or Loratadine 10mg daily. If severe: Prednisolone 40mg x 3-5 days. Identify and avoid triggers.
notes: If anaphylaxis: Epinephrine 0.3mg IM immediately. Prescribe EpiPen if recurrent. Check for underlying cause if chronic >6 weeks.

title: Low back pain
standard_treatment: NSAIDs: Ibuprofen 400mg TID x 5-7 days + Paracetamol 1g QID. Muscle relaxant if spasm: Cyclobenzaprine 5mg TID x 5 days. Stay active, avoid bed rest. Physiotherapy.
notes: X-ray not indicated for acute uncomplicated LBP. MRI only if red flags or no improvement in 6 weeks.

title: Panniculitis, unspecified
standard_treatment: NSAIDs for pain: Ibuprofen 400mg TID. Treat underlying cause. Compression stockings if lower extremity.
notes: Biopsy for definitive diagnosis. Rule out erythema nodosum, infection, malignancy.

title: Urinary tract infection, site not specified
standard_treatment: Uncomplicated: Nitrofurantoin 100mg BID x 5 days or Trimethoprim 200mg BID x 3 days. Increase fluid intake. Cranberry may help prevention.
notes: Urine dipstick and culture. Complicated UTI: 7-14 days antibiotics. Refer urology if recurrent (≥3/year).

title: Benign prostatic hyperplasia with LUTS
standard_treatment: Alpha-blocker: Tamsulosin 0.4mg daily. If prostate large: add Finasteride 5mg daily. Lifestyle: limit fluids before bed, reduce caffeine/alcohol.
notes: Check PSA, DRE, renal function, urinalysis. Post-void residual ultrasound. Refer urology if severe or medication failure.

title: Fever, unspecified
standard_treatment: Acetaminophen 500-1000mg q6h or Ibuprofen 400mg q8h. Increase fluids. Investigate and treat underlying cause.
notes: Check CBC, CRP, blood cultures if >38.5°C. Urinalysis, CXR based on clinical features. Fever of unknown origin: investigate systematically.

title: Headache
standard_treatment: Acetaminophen 1g or Ibuprofen 400mg. Adequate hydration, rest. If tension-type: stress management, physiotherapy.
notes: Distinguish tension vs migraine vs secondary causes. CT/MRI if red flags. Medication overuse headache if analgesics >15 days/month.

title: Abdominal pain, unspecified
standard_treatment: Based on cause. Symptomatic: Hyoscine butylbromide 10mg TID for spasm. Omeprazole 20mg if epigastric. Investigate cause before empiric treatment.
notes: Systematic exam: inspection, auscultation, palpation. Labs: CBC, CRP, amylase, LFTs. Imaging based on clinical suspicion.

title: Cough
standard_treatment: Acute: honey and warm fluids, Dextromethorphan 10-20mg q4h if needed. Chronic >8 weeks: investigate cause — GERD, asthma, post-nasal drip, ACE inhibitor side effect.
notes: CXR if chronic or red flags. Sputum for AFB if TB suspected. PFTs if asthma suspected.

title: Nausea with vomiting
standard_treatment: Ondansetron 4-8mg PO/IV q8h or Metoclopramide 10mg TID before meals. Oral rehydration if tolerating. IV fluids if not. Treat underlying cause.
notes: Check electrolytes, renal function. Consider pregnancy test in women of childbearing age. Investigate persistent vomiting.

title: Acute pharyngitis (sore throat)
standard_treatment: Viral (most common): Acetaminophen 1g q6h, warm salt water gargles, lozenges. If strep confirmed: Amoxicillin 500mg TID x 10 days or Penicillin V 500mg BID x 10 days.
notes: Centor criteria: fever, tonsillar exudate, tender anterior cervical nodes, absence of cough. Rapid strep test if ≥3 criteria.

title: Abnormal glucose
standard_treatment: Prediabetes: lifestyle modification — weight loss 5-7%, exercise 150min/week, diet. Recheck in 3-6 months. If diabetes confirmed: start Metformin 500mg BID.
notes: Confirm with fasting glucose or HbA1c. Prediabetes: FPG 100-125 or HbA1c 5.7-6.4%. Diabetes: FPG ≥126 or HbA1c ≥6.5%.

title: Joint pain
standard_treatment: NSAIDs: Ibuprofen 400mg TID or Diclofenac 50mg BID with food x 7-14 days. Topical NSAIDs for localized pain. Ice/heat therapy. Physiotherapy.
notes: X-ray if trauma or chronic. Joint aspiration if effusion — rule out septic arthritis (emergency), gout, pseudogout. Check uric acid, CRP, ESR.

title: Allergic rhinitis due to pollen
standard_treatment: Intranasal corticosteroid: Fluticasone 2 sprays each nostril daily. Oral antihistamine: Cetirizine 10mg or Loratadine 10mg daily. Avoid allergen exposure.
notes: If severe: consider allergy testing and immunotherapy referral. Eye symptoms: Olopatadine eye drops.

title: Constipation
standard_treatment: Increase fiber 25-30g/day, fluid intake >2L/day. Osmotic laxative: Lactulose 15-30ml daily or Polyethylene glycol 17g daily. Stimulant: Bisacodyl 5-10mg PRN.
notes: Rule out hypothyroidism, hypercalcemia, medications (opioids, anticholinergics). Colonoscopy if alarm features.

title: Candidal stomatitis (oral thrush)
standard_treatment: Nystatin oral suspension 100,000 units/mL: swish and swallow 5mL QID x 7-14 days. If severe: Fluconazole 200mg day 1, then 100mg daily x 7-14 days.
notes: Check for underlying immunosuppression: HIV, diabetes, steroid use, antibiotics. Denture hygiene if applicable.

title: Cellulitis, unspecified
standard_treatment: Mild: Flucloxacillin 500mg QID x 5-7 days or Cefalexin 500mg QID. Mark border of erythema. Elevate affected limb. If penicillin allergic: Clindamycin 300mg QID.
notes: Blood cultures if systemic. IV antibiotics if severe. Rule out DVT if lower limb. Tinea pedis often entry point — treat concurrently.
